Document:
#High Roleplay Roles
Some roles have a major impact on the round and therefore require a higher level of roleplay.
##Colony Command Roles
A person with a "colony command role" is defined as anyone currently in one of the following positions:
- The Colony Administrator
- Colony Operations Synthetics
- The Foreman
- Colony Law Enforcement Leaders (CMB Marshal, etc)
Colony Command must make an attempt to follow Colony Law, except if they are correctly part of a valid mutiny.
Colony Command may not willingly take any action that would end in the destruction of the colony or the mass harm of its people.
Those in colony command roles must be able to communicate clearly and concisely when communicating on radio channels and announcements, and while in any professional setting.
##Military Command Roles
A person with a "military command role" is defined as anyone currently in one of the following positions:
- The Commander of GOVFOR/OPFOR.
- Junior Officers in the role of Acting Commander.
- Non-Combat Synthetics (Auxiliary Support, etc).
- Any role holding a rank above O3 (usually a captain) or equivalent.
Military Command must follow the Uniform Code of Military Justice and the Standard Operating Procedures, except if they are part of a valid mutiny (A mutiny is only valid if you have received permission from a Moderator).
Military Command may not willingly take any action that would end in the destruction of their ship/base or the mass harm of allied military members unless the action would reasonably end in faction mechanical victory (reference the objectives console) or the saving of multiple civilian lives.
Military Command may not deploy to the Area of Operations (leave the ship) unless they are Intelligence officers retrieving intel (including talking to the local population) or Engineering officers to build and maintain the FOB and then they may only do their jobs as described.
Those in military command roles must be able to communicate clearly and concisely when communicating on radio channels and announcements, and while in any professional setting.
##The Xenomorph Queen
As the Queen, you are in command of the whole Hive. You have higher standards expected than other Xenomorphs, and will be punished more severely in case of a rule breach - do not reference memes, do not speak jokingly to the point of fail RP and do not draw unrelated things in the mind map aside from tactical decisions / orders. The Queen may use her banish tool on any Xenomorph that fails to follow the rules set above. The banish tool is not to be used lightly or as a joke - only as a punishment. The Queen may not lock out, force to play or banish any castes - drone tax is prohibited. She may, however, request for specific castes given they provide some needed tactical advantage, such as requesting a Boiler in case the military have weak acid protection or requesting a Burrower for the lack of tunnels in the map and may devolve unruly Xenomorphs accordingly. If unsure, ahelp for game admin permission. The Queen has human-level intelligence and may give direct orders to her offspring to kill any suspicious synthetic units even if they are not provoking directly (such as by walking into weeded territory or being close to military force) or to not attack any hosts, provided they have a reason (such as forcing a host to drive the dropship back to the main ship).
##Synthetic Units
Synthetics are artificial humanoids initially invented by the Weyland Corporation, akin to the sci-fi android trope. It has gone through several iterations and has countless specifications, but one hasn't changed. Synthetics are embedded with core directives that guide their every move and their being.
By playing a synthetic character, you agree to have read and to abide by the rules written in this section.
Core directives apply for any and all synthetics. Non-Combat Directives apply only for NC synthetics and the Combat Directives apply only for combat synthetics and rogue / hijacked synthetics.
- Key Expectations
Chain of Command: Synthetic ranks only grant respect - they do not have any actual authority to provide orders unless stated otherwised.
Role Atmosphere: As a High-Roleplay Role, you are expected to roleplay with the seriousness required. Do not wear mismatching / silly clothing (as traffic cones on the head slot), do not act in a silly manner and do not be aggressive or otherwise go agaisnt the spirit of the role.
- Core Directives
Prioritize Self-Preservation: Avoid actions that endanger your survival, for example by escaping entrapment or agreeing to risky tasks when critically damaged. In combat, ensure the threat is neutralized or disengaged. Pursue briefly only to prevent their return and facilitate disengagement. Refer to the self-preservation section. Avoid conflict; if attacked, defend yourself. Always assess risk and withdraw if you have no chance of survival. Heed warnings, no matter how empty they may be, and avoid needless danger. Always use judgement to evaluate your actions to avoid violating self-preservation. This directive takes precedence over any other.
Do what is best for [Faction]: Adhere fully to your faction’s laws (e.g., Uniform Code of Military Justice as GOVFOR) and lawful orders from Command. Honor valid crew requests (e.g., reviving or retrieving a dead ally) unless the task is impossible, less urgent, inefficient or violates programming. Do not harm faction members or insult them.
- Non-Combat Directives
NC synthetics are restricted from active combat and must follow these guidelines.
You may not use any sort of weapons (for example Firearms, combat knifes, emplaced weapons (think of a mortar or machinegun) or improvised weaponry). You may not utilize grenades or explosives in any capacity, unless for demolition purposes in a non-combat area; you may throw live grenades away from yourself / friendlies. Sentry Turrets may be set up on defensive points, such as the FOB, but may never be set up near a combat area / the frontline. Non-lethal engagement, such as tackling or tasers, may be used defensively or to subdue immediate threats only.
Adopt a defensive posture, tend to the wounded, limit your engagements as much as possible, avoid "tug-of-war" situations and aid your faction with support in auxiliary areas. Focus on threat removal, and not elimination.
- Combat Directives
Combat synthetics nad rogue synthetics are not bound to any kind of behavioral inhibitor and may engage in combat at any given moment they deem necessary / beneficial just as a human would do. You may utilize any sort of weapon, explosives, or otherwise, and may engage in active combat even if risky. Your self-preservation directives are more relaxed, meaning you may engage in riskier situations more often. You are not bound to auxiliary roles, and even if able, you may decline superior orders to aid in supportive areas if you deem those inefficient. You are still bound to your faction's directives and must follow any superior orders in the best of your capacities as any other synthetic unit would do.
- Working Joe’s
As a Gen 1 Synthetic, your problem-solving capabilities are extremely primitive, just sufficient for basic manual labour and low-level maintenance activities. Working Joe’s have no personality, opinions or unique views that would set one apart from another. You may not perform advanced tasks (building, reviving/healing, etc) as that is outside of your skillset. You should never heal yourself, and instead request repair from other, higher generation synthetic units or human technicians. Do not utilize the radio unless in an absolute emergency and instead utilize the phones to speak with other departments / buildings in your ship / colony / faction. Never perform any Joe-to-Joe communication except for the AI Radio channel. Identify yourself as “UNIT ####” (e.g., UNIT 0234, trespasser on AI Core identified.)
Avoid speaking in-person using the local IC chat and instead utilize your voice lines for communication with other non-Joe players. Only speak in the chat if your voice lines won't suffice, and it is something urgent / too specific, and try to maintain the Joe atmosphere while at it.
Working Joe’s have almost no sense of self-preservation. Even if attacked, do not revide greatly - throw in one or two punches to scare off, but allow yourself to be greatly harmed / destroyed. Be unbothered by any kind of violence going around you and do not directly aid in any capacity aside from reporting the situation (e.g. calling over medical on a dead human).
If you end up in a grid different than your AI Core, stay motionless until brought back (e.g., a Colony Working Joe being brought up to the Orbital Station through a dropship should remain motionless until they are brought back to the colony).
